EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system brought heavy snow to much of northern Nevada along with strong gusty winds. Some valleys received 6 to 7 inches of snow with over a foot in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winnemucca received 7 inches of snow and over a foot fell in the mountains.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system brought heavy snow to much of northern Nevada along with strong gusty winds. Some valleys received 6 to 7 inches of snow with over a foot in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Pine Valley 8 inches of snow was reported. The Lewis Peak SNOTEL also received 8 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system brought heavy snow to much of northern Nevada along with strong gusty winds. Some valleys received 6 to 7 inches of snow with over a foot in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A storm system brought heavy snow and strong winds to northern Elko county. Frequent winds gust of 35 to 45 mph were widespread. Many valley locations received 6 to 7 inches of snow while the mountains had 1 to 2 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A storm system dropped 6 to 9 inches of snow across southern Lander and southern Eureka counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Eureka received 8 to 9 inches of new snow and Austin 6 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system brought heavy snow to portions of northern and central Nevada.|The hardest hit area was southern Lander and southern Eureka counties where generally 1 to 2 feet of snow fell.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spring Creek received 6 to 14 inches of snow with the highest amounts adjacent to the Ruby Mountains. In Elko around 5 inches of snow was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system brought heavy snow to portions of northern and central Nevada.|The hardest hit area was southern Lander and southern Eureka counties where generally 1 to 2 feet of snow fell.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ruby Lake reported 11 inches of snow and Currie reported 6 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system brought heavy snow to portions of northern and central Nevada.|The hardest hit area was southern Lander and southern Eureka counties where generally 1 to 2 feet of snow fell.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: SNOTEL sites reported 1 to 2 feet of new snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system brought heavy snow to portions of northern and central Nevada.|The hardest hit area was southern Lander and southern Eureka counties where generally 1 to 2 feet of snow fell.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: At the Cortez mine in Crescent Valley 10 inches of snow was reported, and in Beowawe |6 inches of snow was reported. Emigrant Pass reported 13 inches of snow and the Lewis Peak SNOTEL 17 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system brought heavy snow to portions of northern and central Nevada.|The hardest hit area was southern Lander and southern Eureka counties where generally 1 to 2 feet of snow fell.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Around 2 feet of snow was reported in Austin. In Eureka 14 inches of snow was reported. The Big Creek Summit SNOTEL reported 43 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure moved into Nevada on the 3rd and pushed a strong cold front through the region. The low brought heavy snow (behind the sharp front) to areas near and north of highway 50 in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A widespread 4 to 8 inches of snow fell near and north of highway 50 on the 3rd. Some totals south of Reno were likely enhanced by lake effect snow off of Lake Tahoe.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system brought heavy snow to portions of northern and central Nevada.|The hardest hit area was southern Lander and southern Eureka counties where generally 1 to 2 feet of snow fell.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Between 5 and 10 inches of snow fell in the valleys with 10 to 20 inches in the mountains. Some specific valley amounts include 10 inches in McGill, 9 inches in Ely, and 8 inches at the Moorman Ranch.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell across southwestern Elko county and southern Lander and southern Eureka counties. Reports indicated up to to 8 inches of snow fell in the valleys and 10 to 16 inches in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Spring Creek 5 to 7 inches of snow was reported. Lamoille had 7 inches and Carlin 6 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell across southwestern Elko county and southern Lander and southern Eureka counties. Reports indicated up to to 8 inches of snow fell in the valleys and 10 to 16 inches in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Austin and Eureka reported 8 inches of new snow. SNOTEL's sites reported 10 to 16 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A potent cold front brought strong winds to Humboldt county. Wind gusts up to 72 mph were observed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Morey Creek RAWS reported a gust to 72 mph, the Double H and Texas Springs RAWS had gusts to 59 mph, and the Rose Creek NDOT mesonet site had a gust to 59 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds gusted to 62 mph at the Battle Mountain NDOT mesonet site 6 miles southeast of Battle Mountain.
A thunderstorm wind gust to 61 mph was observed at the Timber Mountain RAWS site.
Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused water up to 6 inches deep to flow through many streets in West Wendover. A wind gust of 62 mph was also observed with this storm.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Weather spotter reported up to 6 inches of water flowing down many streets in West Wendover.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused water up to 6 inches deep to flow through many streets in West Wendover. A wind gust of 62 mph was also observed with this storm.
A thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 64 mph at the Rock Springs Creek RAWS site.
Flash flooding was reported in Lamoille Canyon with water and debris across the road in three separate areas, mainly below the Thomas Canyon campground.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: National Weather Service employees reported three separate areas of water, mud, and debris, which flowed across the road in Lamoille Canyon. Most of the flash flooding was below Thomas Canyon campground.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall from thunderstorms on the Bison Fire scar caused a large debris flow which closed a road in the far western portion of the Smith Valley on the 14th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lyon County officials reported a debris flow (mainly mud/small rocks) which covered Artesia Road to a depth of 3 feet and a width of 200 to 300 feet during the evening of the 14th. The flow was caused by heavy thunderstorm rain on the Bison Fire scar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold low pressure system brought scattered thunderstorms to the Mojave Desert.  A few storms produced small hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nickel size hail covered the ground.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported 2 feet of water flowing across state route 789 near mile marker 6.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported at least 2 feet of water with mud and debris flowing across state route 789 near mile marker 6 with roadway beginning to wash out. Property damage estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A flash flood in Lamoille Canyon covered the road with at least 1 foot of mud and debris.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: NWS employee reported at least 1 foot of mud and debris covered the road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Multiple roads were covered with water or washed out in and around Battle Mountain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Newspaper reports multiple roads covered with water or washed out in and around Battle Mountain. Time estimated based off radar and conversation with paper. Property damage is estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Midas road near Willow Creek Reservoir was washed out by a flash flood.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: COOP Spotter relayed report of washed out road from flash flooding on Midas road near Willow Creek Reservoir. Damages are estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous reports were received of street flooding in and around Winnemucca.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Newspaper relayed numerous reports of street flooding and debris on multiple streets in and around Winnemucca.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A storm system brought up to 2 feet of snow to the mountains and 6 to 12 inches to the valleys in portions of northern and central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Austin received 12 inches of new snow as well as the Vacarro Spring SNOTEL. The Diamond Peak SNOTEL picked up 11 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 64 mph at the Warm Springs Summit CEMP site east of Tonopah near U.S. Highway 6.
A Severe thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 64 mph at the Ruby Valley RAWS site on the east side of the Ruby Mountains.
A severe thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 61 mph at the Timber Mountain RAWS site.
A severe thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 66 mph at the Ely Portable RAWS 12 miles SSE of Schellbourne.
A thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 59 mph at the Baker RAWS site.
A thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 58 mph at the Spruce Mountain RAWS site.
A storm system brought up to 2 feet of snow to the mountains and 6 to 12 inches to the valleys in portions of northern and central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Corral Canyon SNOTEL reported 2 feet of new snow, Dorsey Basin 14 inches, Green Mountain 13 inches, and Lamoille 9 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high winds to portions of the Mojave Desert, downslope winds in the lee of the Sierra Nevada and Spring Mountains, and heavy snow to the southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The peak gust occurred 1 mile ESE of Red Rock Canyon at 1834 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 60 mph at a COOP observer's house 1 mile NNE of McGill.

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high winds to portions of the Mojave Desert, downslope winds in the lee of the Sierra Nevada and Spring Mountains, and heavy snow to the southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A total of 9.9 inches fell in Pioche.

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast moving storm system brought strong winds to portions of the Mojave Desert, and heavy snow to the southern Great Basin and Arizona Strip.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An estimated six inches of snow fell.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Following several nights of subfreezing temperatures, a very cold air mass settled over the Mojave Desert and brought a hard freeze, which damaged some pipes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A few pipes froze in the Las Vegas Valley after several nights of subfreezing temperatures.  The damage was much less than what occurred in a hard freeze eleven months prior.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast moving storm system brought locally heavy snow to the higher terrain of the Sierra Nevada and the Spring Mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Eight inches of snow fell in 6.5 hours.

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong area of low pressure sat over the Desert Southwest for two days, bringing heavy rain and snow to portions of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow in far northern Lincoln County stranded approximately 50 cars on Highway 93, with some drivers trapped for 10-12 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong area of low pressure sat over the Desert Southwest for two days, bringing heavy rain and snow to portions of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain in the Las Vegas Valley contributed to 169 vehicle crashes, with 60 injuries (indirect) and one fatality (indirect).  The heavy rain also caused 2000 customers in the Las Vegas Valley to lose power.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong area of low pressure sat over the Desert Southwest for two days, bringing heavy rain and snow to portions of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Las Vegas Ski and Snowboard Resort measured 24 inches of snow, and the Mount Charleston Fire Department measured 20 inches. Some tree branches were knocked down and power went out.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snow to southern Lander and southern Eureka counties where 6 to 10 inches of snow was reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Austin and Eureka received 6 inches of snow, and the Big Creek Summit SNOTEL reported 10 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hot and dry conditions allowed for very strong outflow winds from thunderstorms in Reno on the 20th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Measured at the NWS Reno office.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought 7 to 10 inches of snow to much of White Pine county with 1 to 2 feet at some mountain locations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snow to White Pine county. Some amounts included:|7 inches at Ely and McGill, 9 inches at Ruth, 8 to 14 inches in the mountains as reported by SNOTEL's, and a whopping 25 inches to the Great Basin National Park Visitor Center.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm dumped heavy snow across Humboldt county, Northern Elko county, and Northern Lander and Northern Eureka counties. Many valley received 5 to 10 inches of snow, with 7 to 14 inches reported by many SNOTEL sites in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snow to much of Humboldt county. In Winnemucca 8 to 11 inches of snow was reported, while 8 to 14 inches was reported by many SNOTEL sites.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm dumped heavy snow across Humboldt county, Northern Elko county, and Northern Lander and Northern Eureka counties. Many valley received 5 to 10 inches of snow, with 7 to 14 inches reported by many SNOTEL sites in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought 5 to 8 inches of snow to much of Northern Lander and northern Eureka counties. Some amounts include 5 to 6 inches in Battle Mountain, 7 inches at the North Area Mine, and 8 inches at Emigrant Pass.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moving across Humboldt county produced wind gusts up to 59 mph and also blew down some trees near Orovada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust of 59 mph was observed at the Texas Springs RAWS and a gust to 58 mph was observed at the Double H RAWS. High winds 3 miles west of Orovada blew down trees and broke gated irrigation pipe and equipment.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds gusted up to 65 mph across south central Elko county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust to 65 mph was observed at the Warm Springs NDOT mesonet site and a gust to 58 mph was recorded at the Pequop Summit NDOT mesonet site.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A storm system brought heavy snow to northern Elko County where up to 10 inches was reported in the mountains and up to 6 inches in the valleys.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several SNOTEL sites reported 8 to 10 inches of snow. The town of Jarbidge and Wild Horse Reservoir reported 6 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported flash flooding on State Highway 278 near mile marker 23 north of Diamond Valley. Road was temporarily closed due to debris on the roadway.
Thunderstorm winds did some property damage at a home near Montello.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind damage from a thunderstorm microburst flipped a medium size horse trailer, bent 1.25 inch metal rods around a horse corral, ripped sections of siding from a trailer and threw it 50 to 100 yards, swamp cooler was thrown 10 feet off the roof, and displaced a camper trailer 1/2 foot from foundation. Other minor damage was reported in the town of Montello. Property damage is estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms produced a wind gust to 59 mph at the Tonopah Airport and a wind gust to 70 mph at the Austin RAWS site northwest of town.
Severe thunderstorms produced a wind gust to 59 mph at the Tonopah Airport and a wind gust to 70 mph at the Austin RAWS site northwest of town.
A severe thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 61 mph at the NDOT mesonet site on Emigrant Pass along Interstate 80.
Lightning sparked the Carpenter One wildfire in the Spring Mountains just outside Las Vegas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning sparked the Carpenter One Wildfire in the Spring Mountains on July 1st. The fire burned a total of 27,881 acres and six structures, and burned well into August before finally being 100% contained.

CZ_NAME: LAKE MEAD/LAKE MOHAVE NATIONAL RECREATION AREA
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large area of high pressure brought record-breaking temperatures to much of the Desert Southwest. Several people died from the heat, many more were injured, and some infrastructure was damaged.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One person died in Laughlin of heat related causes during the prolonged heat wave.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High winds associated with two dissipating showers caused damage in North Las Vegas.  No lightning and little or no rain occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High wind from two dissipating showers blew down trees in North Las Vegas, damaging at least two homes. Several cars were damaged, and some homes lost roof tiles. One fireworks stand also collapsed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Mid level dry air pushed in above low level monsoon moisture, triggering widespread thunderstorms with flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: St. Thomas Road was completely washed out.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: One person died of heat related causes in Las Vegas. The high temperature was only 93, but highs the previous three days had been 106 or higher, with lows in the 80s.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One person in Las Vegas died of heat related causes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A cluster of severe thunderstorms raked the Las Vegas Valley for over an hour, and also produced widespread flash flooding. Numerous trees were blown down, including 243 at the Desert Pines Golf Course. Some trees fell onto and damaged buildings and vehicles, and some ripped out gas and water lines as they were uprooted. Numerous power lines blew down, and several buildings were damaged. The peak measured gust occurred at Nellis AFB at the onset of the event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Mid level dry air pushed in above low level monsoon moisture, triggering widespread thunderstorms with flash flooding and severe weather.
A push of monsoon moisture fueled scattered thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert. Some storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Major flash flooding affected the Carpenter One burn scar. Four feet of water flowed down Harris Springs Canyon, water and debris flowed over and closed Kyle Canyon Road, and mud and debris eventually flowed 20 miles down the mountain onto a few streets in the northwest corner of the Las Vegas Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Daytime heating and climatologically high atmospheric moisture combined to produce strong thunderstorms with heavy rain from the 25th through the 28th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding and a road wash out on highway 338 was reported by Nevada DOT. Several days of thunderstorms with heavy rainfall noted on highway 338 near Sweetwater Summit.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Mid level dry air pushed in above low level monsoon moisture, triggering widespread thunderstorms with flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water up to three feet deep flowed over several streets in Pahrump. Several cars got stuck, and Las Vegas media reported that 20 people were rescued by the Pahrump Fire Department. A spotter measured two inches of rain in an hour and a half.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Mid level dry air pushed in above low level monsoon moisture, triggering widespread thunderstorms with flash flooding and severe weather.
Mid level dry air pushed in above low level monsoon moisture, triggering widespread thunderstorms with flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew down a 20 foot tall palm tree.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Mid level dry air pushed in above low level monsoon moisture, triggering widespread thunderstorms with flash flooding and severe weather.
Mid level dry air pushed in above low level monsoon moisture, triggering widespread thunderstorms with flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm outflow winds knocked down two trees in Boulder City.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Mid level dry air pushed in above low level monsoon moisture, triggering widespread thunderstorms with flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm outflow winds damaged a vehicle by yanking the door out of the owner's hand, bending the hinge.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm dumped up to 1 inch of rain across the western sections of Elko which caused flooding of many streets.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Received numerous reports and pictures from the public and NWS employees of flash flooding on many streets within the city of Elko. Estimated 1 to 2 feet of water flowing across many streets. One basement was reported flooded along with several garages. A mud slide was also reported near Adobe Summit along state route 225 north of Elko.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm coverage increased a little over the Mojave Desert as dry air began to push in above low level monsoon moisture.
Thunderstorm coverage increased a little over the Mojave Desert as dry air began to push in above low level monsoon moisture.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six inches of water flowed down Maryland Pkwy. between Cactus and Silverado Ranch, and in the intersection of Eastern and Silverado Ranch.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A cluster of thunderstorms dumped rain over the Las Vegas Valley for over an hour, and also produced widespread wind damage. Numerous roads were covered with water and debris, there were at least two swift water rescues, and part of the casino floor of Caesars Palace was flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree blew down and landed on power lines, a tent was torn down, a car was flipped over, siding was torn off the Salvation Army building, and a picnic table was thrown over a car.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dust storm conditions reduced visibility to near zero in the northern end of the Las Vegas Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: At least three trees were blown down, a patio cover was blown off a home, a hanging sign was torn from a traffic light, and 6300 customers lost power.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two feet of water flowed down Greenway Road near Mission. Two houses were flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: One person died of heat related causes. The high temperature was 104.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One person in the Las Vegas area died of heat related causes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture continued to fuel isolated thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A report of nickel size hail near Anthem Pkwy. and Bicentennial Pkwy. was relayed through Twitter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One to two feet of water flowed down Harmon Rd. between Sandhill and Boulder Highway. The on-ramp from Flamingo to northbound highway 95 was closed due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sand and small rocks washed onto several roads near Decatur and 215 North, and six inches of water flowed through the intersection of Centennial Pkwy. and 5th Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six inches of water flowed through the intersection of Highways 160 and 372 in Pahrump.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 266 washed out at mile marker 6.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large push of monsoon moisture triggered an extended period of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 264 washed out at mile marker 8.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: One man died in Las Vegas, with heat as a contributing factor, on a day when the temperature reached 108 degrees.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One man died, with heat as a secondary cause of death (indirect fatality) on a day when the temperature reached 108 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Five people died in the Las Vegas area on these three days. High temperatures were 105 or greater each day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Five people died of heat-related causes in the Las Vegas area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A push of monsoon moisture fueled scattered thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert. Some storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 165 was washed out just past the town of Nelson, with 14 cars stranded beyond the washout. The debris flow down the canyon was 100 feet wide and six feet deep. A public bathroom structure was filled halfway with mud.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 61 mph at the Longnow Sagebrush West mesonet site.

A thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 66 mph at the Winnemucca airport. Quarter to near golf ball size hail was reported just southwest of the town of Winnemucca with hail up to quarter size in Winnemucca.
Daytime heating and climatologically high atmospheric moisture combined to produce strong thunderstorms with heavy rain from the 25th through the 28th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm dropped some hail to the size of nickels mixed in with mainly pea-sized hail.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Remnant monsoon moisture continued to fuel scattered thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin, with isolated flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 6 was flooded with water and debris.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Remnant monsoon moisture continued to fuel scattered thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin, with isolated flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 375 was flooded and closed just east of the Nye/Lincoln county line.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 66 mph at the Winnemucca airport. Quarter to near golf ball size hail was reported just southwest of the town of Winnemucca with hail up to quarter size in Winnemucca.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter to near golf ball size hail was observed southwest of Winnemucca near Grass Valley. Hail to the size of quarters damaged RV vents in Winnemucca.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Temperatures reached Excessive Heat Warning criteria for four consecutive days.  Two people died from the heat and eight others were injured.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Temperatures reached Excessive Heat Warning criteria for four consecutive days.  One person died and three others were injured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds and dry conditions helped spread a small brush fire to a home, destroying the home.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A small brush fire, aided by gusty winds and dry conditions, spread to a home near Decatur and Craig Roads in northwest Las Vegas, destroying the home.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large area of high pressure brought record-breaking temperatures to much of the Desert Southwest. Several people died from the heat, many more were injured, and some infrastructure was damaged.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Excessive Heat Warning criteria were reached each day from June 28th through 30th, and continued into July. Hundreds of people were overcome by heat in the Las Vegas Valley, including two fatalities. The heat also knocked out power to 132 customers for less than two hours on the afternoon of the 29th.

CZ_NAME: LAKE MEAD/LAKE MOHAVE NATIONAL RECREATION AREA
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large area of high pressure brought record-breaking temperatures to much of the Desert Southwest. Several people died from the heat, many more were injured, and some infrastructure was damaged.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Excessive Heat Warning criteria were reached each day from June 28th through 30th, and continued into July. Six people were overcome by heat in the Lake Mead National Recreation area, including one fatality.

CZ_NAME: LAKE MEAD/LAKE MOHAVE NATIONAL RECREATION AREA
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated high winds occurred ahead of a strong cold front pushing through the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred at mid-span on the Hoover Dam Bypass bridge.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A band of light freezing rain extending from northeast California into far western Nevada persisted for several hours late on the 20th into the early morning of the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Nevada Highway Patrol reported up to 3 dozen accidents in the Reno area on icy roads caused by freezing rain late on the 20th and early on the 21st. The majority of accidents were on Interstate 580 between Interstate 80 and the Mt Rose exit. Up to 15 vehicles were involved in one incident which closed Interstate 580 for two hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Locally heavy snow fell as a strong upper level low pressure system moved through the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter estimated 12 inches of snow one mile north of Goldfield, with some waist-deep drifts due to strong winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure dropped south from the Pacific Northwest on the 6th and 7th and brought heavy snow to portions of western and west-central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Four to 7 inches of snow fell across northern Pershing County as a cold front moved through the region. Snowfall is estimated based on snow water equivalent from Rye Patch Reservoir and measured snowfall at the Winnemucca airport (Humboldt County).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure dropped south from the Pacific Northwest on the 6th and 7th and brought heavy snow to portions of western and west-central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved through western Nevada early on the 7th bringing 4 to 6 inches of snow on the north and west side of Reno and in the Minden-Gardnerville area. For the Carson City area, 6 to 10 inches of snowfall was reported. The snowfall near Carson City was likely enhanced by lake effect snow bands off of Lake Tahoe.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure dropped south from the Pacific Northwest on the 6th and 7th and brought heavy snow to portions of western and west-central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved through early on the 7th bringing 13 to 17 inches of snowfall to the Carson Range.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure moved into the Pacific Northwest on the 20th, bringing damaging high winds to the Reno and Carson City area ahead of a cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 58 to 66 mph occurred along and west of highway 395 and Interstate 580 from Washoe Valley south in addition to the southwest and northern portions of Reno. In the foothills west of Reno and Carson City, winds gusted between 75 and 81 mph with brief gusts as high as 92 mph on the south end of Washoe Valley around 1430PST. High winds caused power outages in Caughlin Ranch (SW Reno) and Carson City, with power poles knocked down in Carson City. Around 6700 customers were affected by power outages on the afternoon of the 20th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong westerly flow ahead of a storm system brought downslope winds to the Owens Valley and isolated high winds to the southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A dust devil blew over two small travel trailers and damaged a solar panel.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A storm system brought unusually low elevation snow and isolated heavy rain to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Fourteen inches of snow fell at the Las Vegas Ski and Snowboard Resort.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system and cold front brought high winds and dust storm conditions to portions of the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 40 miles E of Beatty.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system and cold front brought high winds and dust storm conditions to portions of the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 3 miles NNW of Centennial Hills.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system and cold front brought high winds and dust storm conditions to portions of the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 1 mile ESE of Red Rock Canyon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds produced a dust storm that reduced visibility to zero at times, causing a 27 vehicle pileup just west of Winnemucca, that resulted in the death of one person and injuring 37 others, 4 seriously.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms winds produced a dust storm that reduced visibility to near zero at times and caused a 27 vehicle pileup on Interstate 80 just west of Winnemucca near mile marker 175. One driver was killed and 37 others were injured, 4 seriously. It took crews 20 hours to clear the debris and reopen Interstate 80. Damages are estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific low pressure system and associated cold front brought high winds to portions of the Mojave Desert.  A couple of thunderstorms also enhanced the winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 5 miles NW of Dyer.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific low pressure system and associated cold front brought high winds to portions of the Mojave Desert.  A couple of thunderstorms also enhanced the winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred at Mountain Springs.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific low pressure system and associated cold front brought high winds to portions of the Mojave Desert.  A couple of thunderstorms also enhanced the winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A light pole was blown over at the intersection of Pecos Rd. and Las Vegas Blvd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific low pressure system and associated cold front brought high winds to portions of the Mojave Desert.  A couple of thunderstorms also enhanced the winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The peak gust was measured 44 miles ENE of Beatty at 0815 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure centered over Utah and eastern Nevada brought hot temperatures to western Nevada on the 28th. The heat set the stage for strong to severe thunderstorms with gusty winds over 50 mph and large hail in the Reno area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail between 0.75 and 1.5 inches in diameter was reported by several spotters in the North Valleys of Reno. The largest hail report was near the Stead airport which had between 1.25 and 1.50 inch diameter hail. Cold Springs (northwest part of hail track) only had hail to 0.75 inches in diameter. Picture evidence of the hail size was sent to the National Weather Service.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure centered over Utah and eastern Nevada brought hot temperatures to western Nevada on the 28th. The heat set the stage for strong to severe thunderstorms with gusty winds over 50 mph and large hail in the Reno area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Five lightning strikes associated with a severe thunderstorm hit the Washoe County Emergency Operations Center's communications tower. The strikes disabled the primary communications repeater although backups were not disabled.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure moved through California and into Nevada from the 9th to the 10th bringing thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong outflow winds to portions of western Nevada during the afternoon and evening of the 10th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain with slow-moving thunderstorms over the Lake Range east of Pyramid Lake caused up to a dozen sections of highway 447 between Nixon and Gerlach to be washed out.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure moved through California and into Nevada from the 9th to the 10th bringing thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong outflow winds to portions of western Nevada during the afternoon and evening of the 10th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Slow-moving thunderstorms with heavy rainfall caused flash flooding over highway 447 about 5 miles northwest of Gerlach. The road was closed due to the flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure moved through California and into Nevada from the 9th to the 10th bringing thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong outflow winds to portions of western Nevada during the afternoon and evening of the 10th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The wind gusts were recorded at the Stagecoach NDOT sensor.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure moved through California and into Nevada from the 9th to the 10th bringing thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong outflow winds to portions of western Nevada during the afternoon and evening of the 10th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This wind gust was recorded at the Juniper Springs RAWS.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure moved through California and into Nevada from the 9th to the 10th bringing thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong outflow winds to portions of western Nevada during the afternoon and evening of the 10th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A Facebook video from the public showed rapidly flowing water over Milan Drive in an industrial area. The water overflowed the drainage ditch alongside the road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure moved through California and into Nevada from the 9th to the 10th bringing thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong outflow winds to portions of western Nevada during the afternoon and evening of the 10th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with torrential rains produced flooding of roads in the north Spanish Springs area with flowing water crossing several residential streets. No significant damage or rescues were noted though. Copious small hail mixed in with the flood waters producing hail drifts of 1-2 feet deep in some yards. Mesonet station nearby (DW4148 Sparks) measured 1.87 inches of rain, with 0.96 inches falling in 16 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure over the northeast Pacific on the 9th dropped into Nevada on the 10th bringing a period of gusty winds ahead of a strong cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds gusted between 40 and 52 mph across much of the area from the afternoon of the 9th through early morning on the 10th. However, Walker Lake RAWS experienced much stronger winds with winds sustained between 38 and 50 mph with gusts between 60 and 73 mph between 0200PST and 0400PST on the 10th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated high wind occurred behind a strong cold front which swept through the southern Great Basin and Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The peak measured gust occurred 39 miles ENE of Beatty.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very cold and dry air mass brought the coldest temperatures in six years across the Mojave Desert.  Hard freezes occurred on four to five consecutive mornings in most places.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several homes in Pahrump had burst pipes due to the cold, and one wellhead also broke. A spotter recorded a low temperature of 11 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very cold and dry air mass brought the coldest temperatures in six years across the Mojave Desert.  Hard freezes occurred on four to five consecutive mornings in most places.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous homes had water pipes burst due to the cold. Several thousand pressure vacuum breakers also broke. Low temperatures on the outskirts of the Las Vegas Valley got down to 17 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very cold low temperatures caused pipe damage in Stateline on the 13th and 14th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several days with lows in the single digits to as low as -14F (South Lake Tahoe airport) and highs in the upper 20s to mid 30s caused pipes to burst at 4 casinos in Stateline on the 13th and 14th. The burst pipes caused water damage to the 18th floor at Harrah's and flooding of casino floors and stores at Harvey's Casino.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flooding of the Health Department building in Duckwater. Debris from flooding made state route 379 impassable between mile marker 4 and 6.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported flooding at the Health Department Building in Duckwater. Debris from flooding made state route 379 impassable between mile marker 4 and 6.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Flooding was observed on O'Neil Basin road by an NDOT road crew.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: NDOT Dispatch reported that crews on highway 93 saw flooding on O'Neil Basin road.|Property damage is estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lingering monsoon moisture continued to fuel thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert. A few storms produced flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning damaged two major distribution circuits, knocking out power to about 2000 people.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lingering monsoon moisture continued to fuel thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert. A few storms produced flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water and debris covered the intersection of Highway 322 and Alexander Ave.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lingering monsoon moisture continued to fuel thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert. A few storms produced flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rain on the Carpenter One burn area caused water and debris to flow across roads in the Rainbow Canyon subdivision.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lingering monsoon moisture continued to fuel thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert. A few storms produced flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water and rocks flowed across Calico Basin Road just north of Highway 159.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system passing by to the north produced gusty winds over the Mojave Desert. One station recorded a brief high wind gust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 40 miles E of Beatty.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds up to 60 mph blew some siding off of a structure and blew a fence down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: COOP observer estimated wind gusts to 60 mph caused some siding to come off a structure and blew a fence down. Damages are estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds up to 60 mph blew some siding off of a structure and blew a fence down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pilot Peak Junction Mesonet site measured a thunderstorm wind gust to 60 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding caused a significant washout along the road through the Pancake mountain range north of Duckwater.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency manager and Duckwater Health Department reported flash flooding along White Pine County Road north of town through the Pancake mountain range which caused a significant washout in the road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong area of low pressure brought high downslope winds to the Spring Mountains and locally strong winds to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 3 miles SSW of Mercury.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong area of low pressure brought high downslope winds to the Spring Mountains and locally strong winds to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds blew down a mesquite tree 3 miles WNW of North Las Vegas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong area of low pressure brought high downslope winds to the Spring Mountains and locally strong winds to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The peak gust was measured 5 miles NW of Goodsprings at 1134 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A flash flood occurred about 8 miles south of Lund on state route 318 with a 1/2 mile wide area of water and debris flowing across the road.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Department of Highways and law enforcement reported a flash flood occurred along state route 318 near mile marker 6 with one to one and a half feet of water, mud, large pine trees, and other debris flowing across the roadway. The road was impassable and the debris was cleared with a snowplow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure dropping into the Pacific Northwest pushed a strong cold front through western Nevada on the 24th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Tree branches 4 to 6 inches in diameter were blown onto mobile home roof. The roof of the mobile home was penetrated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system produced strong winds over the Mojave Desert. One station recorded a brief high wind gust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 39 miles ENE of Beatty.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific storm system brought high winds to the mountains and deserts of southeast California and southern Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The top of a large Ponderosa pine blew off and fell onto a fence on Mt. Charleston, two Porta-Potties were blown over at Spring Mountain Ranch State Park, and power was knocked out to 72 customers in Goodsprings. The peak gust was measured at the Las Vegas Ski and Snowboard Resort at 0700 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific storm system brought high winds to the mountains and deserts of southeast California and southern Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The peak gust was measured 40 miles E of Beatty at 0200 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific storm system brought high winds to the mountains and deserts of southeast California and southern Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power was knocked out to 600 customers in Indian Springs. The peak gust was measured 1 mile N of Indian Springs at 0104 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific storm system brought high winds to the mountains and deserts of southeast California and southern Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several trees were blown down, a large gate was broken, a carport was toppled, and about 1800 customers lost power. The peak gust was measured 16 miles NW of North Las Vegas at 0524 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure off the central California coast contributed to the formation of thunderstorms in western Nevada on the 20th and 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 3-inch diameter apple tree and 30 feet of fence was blown over due to thunderstorm outflow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure off the central California coast contributed to the formation of thunderstorms in western Nevada on the 20th and 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Measured at Juniper Springs RAWS station.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure off the central California coast contributed to the formation of thunderstorms in western Nevada on the 20th and 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Measured at Walker Lake RAWS. Time of gust between 1739PST and 1759PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lingering monsoon moisture from a strong push in late August continued to fuel thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert through early September. Several storms produced flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain on the Carpenter One burn area produced major flash flooding. Several homes in the Rainbow subdivision on Mount Charleston were damaged by flowing water and debris estimated to be five feet deep, and over 30 residents had to be helped from their homes. Highway 157 was closed due to flooding in the Rainbow Canyon area. Highway 160 was closed at Tecopa Road due to flooding. Several inches of water and mud flowed over roads in the area of Grand Teton and Hualapai Way in the northwest corner of the Las Vegas Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lingering monsoon moisture from a strong push in late August continued to fuel thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert through early September. Several storms produced flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six to 18 inches of water with mud and debris flowed down Sunset Road from Mountain Vista to Arroyo Grande.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lingering monsoon moisture from a strong push in late August continued to fuel thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert through early September. Several storms produced flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Multiple low water crossings on Cottonwood Cove Road were filled with swift water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lingering monsoon moisture from a strong push in late August continued to fuel thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert through early September. Several storms produced flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: About 9 inches of mud covered the road to Valley of Fire State Park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large area of high pressure brought record-breaking temperatures to much of the Desert Southwest. Several people died from the heat, many more were injured, and some infrastructure was damaged.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Excessive Heat Warning criteria were reached each day from the 1st through the 4th.  Hundreds of people were overcome by the heat. An unusually high mortality rate persisted until July 11th, by which time 39 people had died (including two in June). Another man died in September after being found unresponsive on July 5th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lingering monsoon moisture continued to fuel thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert. A few storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two to three inches of water, mud, and rocks washed across the 215 Beltway at Ann Road, and also over Cliff Shadows Parkway between Lone Mountain and Alexander.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lingering monsoon moisture continued to fuel thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert. A few storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.

A winter storm dumped heavy snow across Humboldt county, Northern Elko county, and Northern Lander and Northern Eureka counties. Many valley received 5 to 10 inches of snow, with 7 to 14 inches reported by many SNOTEL sites in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snow to much of northern Elko county. Some valley locations received 6 to 9 inches of snow, while many SNOTEL's reported 7 to 13 inches of snow. Some amounts included 6 inches at Tuscarora, and 9 inches at Mountain city.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another surge of monsoon moisture fueled widespread thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain on the Carpenter One burn area led to major flash flooding. Trout Canyon Road was destroyed, and a nearly completed water line was destroyed as well. Highway 160 was closed near the Tecopa turnoff due to water and mud on the road. The next morning, there was standing water seven feet deep in the highway median, with trees, mud, ash, and debris mixed in.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another surge of monsoon moisture fueled widespread thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water three feet deep flowed across Dean Martin Drive at Cactus Avenue, and the intersection of Cactus and Bermuda was also impassable.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another surge of monsoon moisture fueled widespread thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain on the Carpenter One burn area led to three feet of water flowing at the intersection of Grand Canyon and Grand Teton.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another surge of monsoon moisture fueled widespread thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six inches of water flowed across Main Street in Panaca. Flooding also caused significant damage to crops on several farms west of Panaca.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another surge of monsoon moisture fueled widespread thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck and set fire to a house in the Southern Highlands neighborhood.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another surge of monsoon moisture fueled widespread thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two feet of mud covered Highway 266 near Lida Pass.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another surge of monsoon moisture fueled widespread thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water entered the basement of a home in Pioche, damaging two bedrooms. Rocks and debris accumulated on several streets. 2.73 inches of rain fell in two hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported flash flooding washed out sections of Greystone mine road.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported that flash flooding washed out sections of Greystone mine road. Property damage is estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds spread a grass fire to two homes in Las Vegas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds spread a grass fire to two homes and two vehicles. Two people were injured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture began to return to the Mojave Desert, fueling thunderstorms. Some storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water overtopped a small bridge near the Fletcher View Trailhead just south of Mount Charleston, rendering the bridge impassable. Harris Springs Road was also impassable at mile marker 8 due to flooding. Up to a foot of debris was deposited around picnic tables in the Kyle Canyon picnic area. Ash filled water flowed through several yards in the Rainbow subdivision.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture began to return to the Mojave Desert, fueling thunderstorms. Some storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water flowed over a road near the Jean Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture began to return to the Mojave Desert, fueling thunderstorms. Some storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six to seven inches of water flowed through the intersection of Serene Ave. and Maryland Pkwy.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another surge of monsoon moisture fueled widespread thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 159 was closed at Highway 160 due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another surge of monsoon moisture fueled widespread thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck and exploded a transformer.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Near record to record high temperatures with strong to severe thunderstorms caused strong outflow winds with dust storms, large hail and very heavy rain from the 1st through the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Lovelock ASOS reported visibility as low as 1/4 mile with between 1/4 and 1/2 mile between about 1730PST and 1815PST. The low visibilities likely affected Interstate 80. Thunderstorms caused widespread wind gusts between 40-50 mph in Pershing, Churchill and northern Lyon Counties.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture began to return to the Mojave Desert, fueling thunderstorms. Some storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: At least six inches of water flowed across Sunridge Heights Parkway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture began to return to the Mojave Desert, fueling thunderstorms. Some storms produced flash flooding and severe weather.
Another surge of monsoon moisture fueled widespread thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water, mud, and small rocks flowed through the parking lot of the Searchlight Nugget casino.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two people died in Clark County of heat related causes. High temperatures were 104 to 106 degrees.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two people died in the Las Vegas area of heat related causes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two people died of heat stress on a day with a high temperature of 104 degrees.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two men died of heat stress.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two people died of heat stress on days with high temperatures of 106 and 109 degrees.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two people died of heat stress.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Near record to record high temperatures with strong to severe thunderstorms caused strong outflow winds with dust storms, large hail and very heavy rain from the 1st through the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts 59 to 66 mph were reported at the Walker Lake NDOT sensor.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Near record to record high temperatures with strong to severe thunderstorms caused strong outflow winds with dust storms, large hail and very heavy rain from the 1st through the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gust measured at NWS Reno.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another surge of monsoon moisture fueled widespread thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew a power pole down onto a house.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Near record to record high temperatures with strong to severe thunderstorms caused strong outflow winds with dust storms, large hail and very heavy rain from the 1st through the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm outflows of 50-60 mph caused blowing dust which reduced visibilities down to 1/8 mile in Fernley and Fallon with 3/4 mile at the Lovelock airport. The 60 mph gust was recorded at the Fallon Naval Air Station. A Facebook photo near Lovelock clearly showed a wall of dust (haboob).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Near record to record high temperatures with strong to severe thunderstorms caused strong outflow winds with dust storms, large hail and very heavy rain from the 1st through the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail between 0.88 and 1 inch.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Near record to record high temperatures with strong to severe thunderstorms caused strong outflow winds with dust storms, large hail and very heavy rain from the 1st through the 4th.
Near record to record high temperatures with strong to severe thunderstorms caused strong outflow winds with dust storms, large hail and very heavy rain from the 1st through the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three feet of standing water was reported on Scott Drive in Fernley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong push of monsoon moisture fueled thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin for several days, peaking on the 25th. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
Near record to record high temperatures with strong to severe thunderstorms caused strong outflow winds with dust storms, large hail and very heavy rain from the 1st through the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Lyon County sheriff reported house and yard flooding near Saddlehorn Way, Mallard Way and Jenny's Lane. In addition, the Wendy's in central Fernley noted flooding. Three waves of heavy thunderstorm rainfall produced up to 1.3 inches of rain (per spotter reports) between about 1500PST and 1900PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Near record to record high temperatures with strong to severe thunderstorms caused strong outflow winds with dust storms, large hail and very heavy rain from the 1st through the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Interstate 80 was closed between exits 43 and 46 between about 1600PST and 1730PST due to very heavy rain on the freeway causing a large amount of ponding water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Near record to record high temperatures with strong to severe thunderstorms caused strong outflow winds with dust storms, large hail and very heavy rain from the 1st through the 4th.
Near record to record high temperatures with strong to severe thunderstorms caused strong outflow winds with dust storms, large hail and very heavy rain from the 1st through the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Measured at the Majuba mesonet site.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Near record to record high temperatures with strong to severe thunderstorms caused strong outflow winds with dust storms, large hail and very heavy rain from the 1st through the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Training thunderstorms brought very heavy rain to the Fernley area during the afternoon and evening of the 4th. Flash flooding caused a 10 ft sinkhole to form near the intersection of Cable Canyon Rd and Seabiscuit Dr.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong push of monsoon moisture fueled thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin for several days, peaking on the 25th. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several inches of water with rocks and debris flowed over Pahrump Road near Sandy Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong push of monsoon moisture fueled thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin for several days, peaking on the 25th. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.

A strong push of monsoon moisture fueled thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin for several days, peaking on the 25th. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rain on the Carpenter One burn area produced major flash flooding. 18 swift water rescues took place. Numerous roads were closed by flooding, including Kyle Canyon Road (which was washed away) and Highway 95. Water, mud, and firefighting foam covered many streets in northwest Las Vegas for three days as the Kyle Canyon Detention Basin slowly drained. Much of the Las Vegas Paiute Golf Resort was heavily damaged.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong push of monsoon moisture fueled thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin for several days, peaking on the 25th. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several roads were flooded in the Laughlin area, with one swift water rescue reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hot and dry conditions allowed for very strong outflow winds from thunderstorms in Reno on the 20th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts between 60 and 77 mph were recorded at the NWS Reno office. The source of the wind gusts was not clear as it could have been from a nearby shower or a larger scale outflow from thunderstorms in the Sierra.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 65 mph at a mesonet site on the west side of the Carlin Tunnel on Interstate 80.
Flashing flooding in Carlin Canyon caused a rock slide that closed the Interstate 80 detour through that area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local law enforcement reports Interstate 80 in the Carlin Canyon is closed due to flash flooding and small rock slide.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 375 was washed out at Lincoln County mile marker 23.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew the roof off a hangar at the North Las Vegas Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong push of monsoon moisture fueled thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin for several days, peaking on the 25th. Many storms produced flash flooding, and isolated severe weather also occurred.
